chapter_title.ar: باب كَرَاهَةِ التَّعَرُّضِ لِمَا لَا يُطِيقُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – Abhorrence of exposing oneself to what one cannot endure

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/412)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/412)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) لَا يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُؤْمِنِ أَنْ يُذِلَّ نَفْسَهُ قُلْتُ بِمَا يُذِلُّ نَفْسَهُ قَالَ يَدْخُلُ فِيمَا يَتَعَذَّرُ مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Mufazzal Bin Umar who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is not befitting for the Believer that he humiliates himself’. I said, ‘With what does he humiliate himself?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He indulges in what he cannot do from his (resources)’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from his father from Muhammad ibn Sinan from Mufaddal ibn ‘Umar who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘A believer must not humiliate himself.’ I then asked, ‘In what way can he humiliate himself?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘He can do so by involving himself in things that he cannot do.’”
